subject: Re: M crashes when creating species
body:
Thanks, Claris!

Reaffirms our suspicion that the error originates from how the Windows 
machine communicates with our data storage server.

Working with everything locally will have to be our interim solution as 
well.
Will report back to the google group if we can identify what aspect of the 
data storage server is the root of the problem.

-Tom


On Sunday, February 6, 2022 at 9:13:26 PM UTC-8 cla...@anaphase.org wrote:

> Hi Thomas,
>
> We were also encountering the error intermittently but it stopped when we 
> stored *all *M files locally on the Windows PC (including the input files 
> required for species creation), instead of one of our servers. Since then, 
> we have not encountered the error at all. We're not sure why but we're fine 
> with storing the files locally so we've stuck with that.
>
> I hope this helps. Good luck!
>
> Best,
> Claris
>
>
> On Mon, Feb 7, 2022 at 9:03 AM thomas.la...@gmail.com <
> thomas.la...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Claris,
>>
>> Were you (or anyone else for that matter) able to find a solution to this 
>> error?
>>
>> We have been encountering the same error *sometimes*.
>> Deleting the new species folder (if any) and attempting to the same steps 
>> to create a new species, which then *sometimes* completes without error.
>>
>> We also sometimes are able to create a species, but then encounter this 
>> error after completing a refinement cycle.
>> All of the files are written out for the refinement cycle, but attempting 
>> to continue results in a different error.
>>
>> Our group switched recently our data storage configuration, and thus we 
>> are trying to determine whether an aspect of the reconfiguration may be the 
>> cause.
>> The main bottleneck in trouble-shooting with our IT staff is that the 
>> error isn't reproducible...
>>
>> Any advice will be greatly appreciated for this case as well!
>>
>> Best,
>> -Tom  
>>
>>
>> On Sunday, November 7, 2021 at 8:44:57 PM UTC-8 cla...@anaphase.org 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi everyone,
>>>
>>> I've been trying to create a species in M but it keeps crashing after

>>> thanks in advance!
